excerpts.text: The normal Christian life is the life of the Lord Jesus lived out in the believer by the power of the Holy Spirit.
God's way of deliverance is altogether different from man's way. Man's way is to try to suppress sin by seeking to overcome it; God's way is to remove the sinner.
Our old man has been crucified with Christ. This is not something we must do, but something that has been done. We must simply reckon it so.
Man is a being of three parts — spirit, soul, and body. God dwells in the spirit; the self dwells in the soul; the senses dwell in the body.
The cross is not merely the place of forgiveness; it is the place of death. We must not only receive forgiveness but also accept the sentence of death upon the old man.
The church in the New Testament is always local. It is the church in Ephesus, the church in Corinth. There is no universal church organization — only local assemblies.
Christianity is not a set of doctrines; it is Christ Himself living in us. Apart from Him, we have nothing.
I was saved at the age of seventeen through the preaching of Dora Yu. From that moment, I knew that my life was not my own.
To hold a thing fast is to lose it. To let it go is to save it. The river of living water must flow; it cannot be dammed.
The blood of Christ deals with what we have done; the Cross deals with what we are. The blood procures our pardon; the Cross procures our deliverance.
We do not consecrate ourselves in order to be holy; we consecrate ourselves because we are already holy. Consecration is not a means; it is a response.
I have decided to follow the Lord. Though the way ahead is unclear, I have no intention of turning back.
The soulish believer is one who lives by his own power, his own wisdom, his own feelings. The spiritual believer is one who lives by the Spirit alone.
God does not want us to do anything for Him. He wants us to be something before Him. Being must precede doing.
We must first sit with Christ in the heavenlies, then walk worthy of our calling, and finally stand against the enemy. The order cannot be reversed.
The denominational system is a human invention. In the Bible, there is one church in one city. Any division beyond this is sectarian.
I have been in prison now for many years. I maintain my joy. Grace sustains me.
God never asks us to do anything that we can do. He only asks us to do what we cannot do, so that His power may be displayed.
By 1928 I had written The Spiritual Man. I was twenty-five years old. Looking back, I see that God gave me those truths far beyond my experience.
I established the Local Church assemblies according to the New Testament pattern. No denomination, no foreign mission board. Just believers gathering in the name of the Lord.
Brothers and sisters, we are not called to build an organization. We are called to express the Body of Christ in our locality.
The government has accused me of many things. I will not defend myself. My Lord was silent before His accusers, and so shall I be.
Revelation is the first step; faith is the second. God opens our eyes to see a fact; then we take our stand upon it.
I have served the Lord for over thirty years. I am now in chains. But God's Word is not chained.
Christ has died; Christ has risen; Christ is now in the heavenlies. This is our gospel, and we stand upon nothing else.
